spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (PerformanceObserver) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (5 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (auto, becomes, container, content, each, entries, sections, timing, toggle, visibility, visible, watch).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "The Container Timing API enables monitoring when annotated sections of the DOM are displayed on screen and have finished their initial paint. A developer will have the ability to mark subsections of t". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("The Container Timing API enables monitoring when annotated sections of the DOM are displayed on screen and have finished their initial paint. A developer will have the ability to mark subsections of the DOM with the containertiming attribut") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://WICG.github.io/container-timing to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~9751 chars of script across 5 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
